subject: OOF HAS Helm chart updates
commit_message:
OOF HAS Helm chart updates

Helm charts updated for HAS with MUSIC v3.
Sync conductor.conf, added dependent containers for AAF.
Read config from values.yaml.
Disabled AAF authentication.
Updated resources for OSDF and HAS charts.
Removed hardcoded values for namespace

Change-Id: I683cd0b8c86a6cc9a4b4c4793051878e0d0e5b2f
Issue-ID: OPTFRA-363
Signed-off-by: Dileep Ranganathan <dileep.ranganathan@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Kiran Kamineni <kiran.k.kamineni@intel.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/755/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/755

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

OPTFRA-299 is more about hardening I guess (and seems to be a closed issue). Just curious, why are we adding AAF here? Can we create a different issue / tasks with better details in it?
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

> OPTFRA-299 is more about hardening I guess (and seems to be a
 > closed issue). Just curious, why are we adding AAF here? Can we
 > create a different issue / tasks with better details in it?

aaf-service is needed for AAF RBAC and authentication,  aaf-sms is required for Secret Management Service. OPTFRA-299 was closed sooner I guess. Anyway I have created another task for this under the epic for hardening as I couldn't find a better suited Epic in the list. Please suggest if this should be associated with another Epic.

Patch Set 27:

Overall OOF is requesting 6GO of RAM and 3000m CPUs with the "small" flavor, it seems to be a lot to me. I strongly think these numbers should be revisted.
